glossary.definition: Rivetz's vision for a digital future built on verifiable trust, where every interaction has provable origin and integrity
Global Attestation and Identity Network — a framework combining trusted computing, blockchain, and tokenization for provable, tamper-proof digital interactions
Real-time integrity verification of devices and systems using hardware-based cryptographic proof (TPM, TEE, HSM)
The utility token powering Digital 2.0 operations — used for device allowances, policy enforcement, service payments, and cross-chain operations
A cryptographically signed record of security controls, policies, and compliance verification that can be validated by any party
Patented technology developed with Telefonica combining two independent hardware security foundations for enhanced verification
A decentralized system for establishing, verifying, and managing digital identities and permissions using blockchain-anchored cryptographic proof
Hardware-based cryptographic attestation ensuring every device in the ecosystem is uniquely identifiable
